title: Interim Clinical Considerations for Use of Vaccine for Monkeypox Prevention in the United States | Monkeypox | CDC
text:

Either the standard (0.5mL subcutaneous) or the alternative (0.1mL ID) regimen may be used. There is currently adequate supply of JYNNEOS vaccine. Therefore, clinicians can preferentially administer JYNNEOS via the subcutaneous route.

1 For patients less than 6 months of age, Vaccinia Immune Globulin Intravenous (VIGIV) should be considered in lieu of JYNNEOS vaccine. Clinicians should first contact their jurisdictional health department ( Jurisdictional Contacts ). Jurisdictional health departments can facilitate consultation with CDC and access to VIGIV.
Duration of immunity
